Drops/PARMA models performance of generalized LLM computation on vectorized CGRAs, accounting for non-linear functions and memory hierarchy. 2604.07526 uses RL to co-optimize ASIC architecture and software compilation for on-device inference. 2604.09631 studies edge object detection fault injection, measuring hardware utilization under degraded conditions. 2604.09752 and 2604.09083 address memory-bound and latency-bound scenarios respectively. The convergence is toward hardware-software co-design and customized architectures for inference.
  • From LLM to Silicon: RL-Driven ASIC Architecture Exploration for On-Device AI Inference [#50, Innovative]
  • Hardware Utilization and Inference Performance of Edge Object Detection Under Fault Injection [#185, Incremental]

LLM Agents and Autonomous Systems — avg: 51 (Moderately Innovative)

These three papers address coordination and instruction handling in multi-agent LLM systems. Multi-User LLM Agents tackles multi-principal scenarios with conflicting objectives and role-based authority. Many-Tier Instruction Hierarchy formalizes privilege levels across heterogeneous instruction sources. LLM Powered Autonomous Agents provides a comprehensive architectural survey across vision, NLP, and robotics communities. The papers suggest maturing frameworks for autonomous agent design beyond single-user, single-objective paradigms.
  • Multi-User Large Language Model Agents [#42, Innovative]
  • Many-Tier Instruction Hierarchy in LLM Agents [#66, Innovative]
  • Large Language Model Powered Autonomous Agents: Architecture, Collaboration, and Applications [#220, Routine]

Healthcare and Clinical AI Systems — avg: 50 (Moderately Innovative)

These six papers apply AI to clinical and biomedical workflows. Multi-agent AI for Preventive Health synthesizes heterogeneous examination findings into actionable risk assessment. K-STAMM uses knowledge-enhanced spatial-temporal models for pneumonia prediction. MeSH-Augmented Embeddings improve biomedical document retrieval with medical ontology. Integrating Clinical Evidence addresses multi-condition care beyond single-disease guidelines. NFR Framework formalizes non-functional requirements for trustworthy healthcare AI. NovoExpert-2 predicts ADMET properties for drug discovery. The group reflects AI's evolution from single-task diagnostics toward integrated, multi-condition, evidence-based clinical workflows.
  • Uncertainty-quantified Pulse Signal Recovery from Facial Video using Regularized Stochastic Interpolants [#21, Highly Innovative]
  • Clinically grounded multi-agent artificial intelligence for preventive health management [#82, Innovative]
  • Integrating clinical evidence for multi-condition care [#114, Moderately Innovative]
  • NovoExpert-2: State-of-the-Art ADMET Prediction via Gradient-Boosted Trees on MapLight Fingerprints and GIN Embeddings [#167, Moderately Innovative]
  • K-STAMM: A Knowledge-Enhanced Spatial–Temporal Attention Model with Multimodal Fusion for Pneumonia Prediction [#177, Moderately Innovative]
  • A knowledge-driven semantic framework for non-functional requirement engineering in intelligent healthcare systems [#191, Incremental]
  • Benchmarking MeSH-Augmented Embeddings for Biomedical Document Similarity [#203, Incremental]

Token Efficiency & Inference Cost Optimization — avg: 50 (Moderately Innovative)

These papers address the growing token consumption and inference cost of LLM systems. 2604.03044 (JoyAI-Flash) tackles excessive token consumption in mid-scale models. 2603.29919 (SkillReducer) shows that agent skills systematically increase token overhead, proposing optimization strategies. 2604.07853 (QaRL) quantizes rollout models while maintaining RL training stability, reducing generation cost by ~40%. 2604.08075 (Dual-Pool Routing) observes that most requests fit under 8K tokens yet systems configure for 64K, proposing dynamic allocation. 2604.10152 (SpecMoE) uses speculative decoding to reduce MoE memory bandwidth. Collectively, they reveal substantial efficiency gains from matching compute to actual workload characteristics.
  • SkillReducer: Optimizing LLM Agent Skills for Token Efficiency [#58, Innovative]
  • SpecMoE: A Fast and Efficient Mixture-of-Experts Inference via Self-Assisted Speculative Decoding [#84, Innovative]
  • JoyAI-LLM Flash: Advancing Mid-Scale LLMs with Token Efficiency [#102, Innovative]
  • QaRL: Rollout-Aligned Quantization-Aware RL for Fast and Stable Training under Training–Inference Mismatch [#125, Moderately Innovative]
  • Dual-Pool Token-Budget Routing for Cost-Efficient and Reliable LLM Serving [#148, Moderately Innovative]
  • Efficient Optimization of Large Language Models via Parameter-Efficient Tuning and Adaptive Inference [#222, Routine]

LLM Inference System Architecture — avg: 50 (Moderately Innovative)

These papers optimize the systems layer of LLM inference—scheduling, batching, parallelism, and energy. 2604.12171 (PipeLive) dynamically reconfigures pipeline parallelism to adapt to workload shifts. 2604.11948 applies imitation learning to CPU LFM inference on many-cores with HBM constraints. 2604.09722 (ConfigSpec) profiles and selects deployment configurations for edge-cloud speculative serving. 2604.09048 measures energy consumption across heterogeneous GPUs, revealing hardware-workload dependencies. 2604.07472 jointly optimizes model selection, GPU provisioning, and workload routing under SLO constraints. 2604.06664 (Foundry) templates CUDA graph materialization to reduce cold-start latency. The field is moving from static deployments toward adaptive, multi-tenant systems.
  • Foundry: Template-Based CUDA Graph Context Materialization for Fast LLM Serving Cold Start [#34, Innovative]
  • PipeLive: Efficient Live In-place Pipeline Parallelism Reconfiguration for Dynamic LLM Serving [#107, Moderately Innovative]
  • Fast Heterogeneous Serving: Scalable Mixed-Scale LLM Allocation for SLO-Constrained Inference [#144, Moderately Innovative]
  • Active Imitation Learning for Thermal- and Kernel-Aware LFM Inference on 3D S-NUCA Many-Cores [#162, Moderately Innovative]
  • Watt Counts: Energy-Aware Benchmark for Sustainable LLM Inference on Heterogeneous GPU Architectures [#176, Moderately Innovative]
  • ConfigSpec: Profiling-Based Configuration Selection for Distributed Edge–Cloud Speculative LLM Serving [#189, Incremental]

Tensor Algebra and Numerical Computing — avg: 50 (Moderately Innovative)

All four papers optimize core numerical operations for modern workloads. STTID extends tensor-train decomposition to sparse inputs by preserving sparsity structure. HaCOO provides flexible sparse tensor storage with rapid insertion and access patterns. Symmetric SpMV exploits matrix symmetry to reduce memory footprint and bandwidth. GPVI introduces internal clustering validity indices without ground truth labels. The group represents optimization of fundamental computational kernels for sparse, dynamic, and unsupervised data regimes.
  • STTID: High-Performance Sparse Tensor-Train Interpolative Decomposition [#44, Innovative]
  • Towards Efficient Symmetric Sparse Matrix-Vector Multiplication on Multi-Cores [#131, Moderately Innovative]
  • GPVI: An Internal Index Based on Grouping Partition for Clustering Validity Validation [#178, Moderately Innovative]
  • HaCOO: Hashed Coordinate Sparse Tensor Storage for Rapid Data Insertion, Access, and Updates [#193, Incremental]

Human-AI Interaction and Collaborative Systems — avg: 50 (Moderately Innovative)

These papers investigate how humans interact with and evaluate AI systems. User Interventions shows that full autonomy is error-prone; successful deployments require human oversight mechanisms. Evolving Enactions reveals how AI coding assistants reshape expertise evaluation in hiring and technical assessment. AI That Moves With You explores assistive technologies for accessibility. Surface vs Structural Similarity investigates alignment between computational metrics and human judgments. Together they emphasize that AI integration requires understanding human agency, expertise, and decision-making.
  • Evolving Enactions of Expertise: Software Engineers' Evaluation and Demonstration of Coding Expertise with AI Coding Assistants [#95, Innovative]
  • "Here, Let Me Help": An Empirical Study of User Interventions in Human–Web Agent Collaboration [#124, Moderately Innovative]
  • Surface vs. Structural Similarity: Aligning Computational Similarity Metrics with Human Judgments [#171, Moderately Innovative]
  • AI That Moves With You: A Review of Interactive Technologies Powered by Large Foundation Models for Mobility Impairment [#187, Incremental]

Hardware Acceleration and Processor Architectures — avg: 50 (Moderately Innovative)

These papers address compute architectures for emerging workloads. MT-3000 Attention adapts FlashAttention kernels to many-core processors lacking GPU primitives. FlexVector designs a GCN-specific processor with flexible VRF to handle power-law degree distributions. Symphony Compute Ontology frames Kubernetes and OpenShift as compute ontologies rather than mere container schedulers. The papers reflect hardware-software co-design toward domain-specific acceleration and semantic compute models.
  • Optimizing Attention for Large Language Model Inference on the MT-3000 Many-Core Processor [#86, Innovative]
  • FlexVector: A SpMM Vector Processor with Flexible VRF for GCNs on Varying-Sparsity Graphs [#140, Moderately Innovative]
  • Symphony as Compute Ontology: Extending Insight into OpenShift and NVIDIA AI Factories [#194, Incremental]

Knowledge Graphs for AI Systems — avg: 49 (Moderately Innovative)

These papers address knowledge graph construction, quality assessment, and application to AI systems. 2604.12025 (WiseOWL) proposes systematic criteria for ontology selection and reuse, bridging knowledge engineering best practices with ML. Arxiv/graph-survey comprehensively reviews graph-enhanced LLMs, showing that explicit relational structure improves factuality and reasoning. Vldb/GED proposes efficient graph edit distance estimation for KG similarity. Springer/KG-EHR integrates heterogeneous EHR standards (ICD, LOINC) into a unified KG for clinical AI. 2604.10384 (Context-KG) visualizes KGs with user context and ontological guidance, solving the bootstrapping problem (must understand KG to query it). Springer/KG4CUT builds domain-specific ontology for cutting tool selection. OQuaRE-KG adapts quality frameworks from ontology engineering to KG evaluation. The convergence is toward domain-specific, quality-assured KGs as structured knowledge substrates for LLM reasoning.
  • Context-KG: Context-Aware Knowledge Graph Visualization with User Preferences and Ontological Guidance [#103, Innovative]
  • WiseOWL: A Methodology for Evaluating Ontological Descriptiveness and Semantic Correctness for Ontology Reuse and Ontology Recommendations [#186, Incremental]

Retrieval-Augmented Generation and Reasoning — avg: 47 (Moderately Innovative)

Both papers enhance retrieval systems with reasoning capabilities. TM-RAG addresses long-form evidence aggregation using Transformer-Mamba architectures to maintain stable global representations. Graph-Enhanced Fake News Detection combines graph structure with LLMs for explainable veracity classification. They suggest RAG systems are evolving from simple vector retrieval toward hybrid architectures that combine dense retrieval, structured reasoning, and user-interpretable explanations.
  • A Graph-Enhanced Defense Framework for Explainable Fake News Detection with LLM [#121, Moderately Innovative]
  • TM-RAG: A Transformer-Mamba Model for Long-Text Evidence Aggregation in Retrieval-Augmented Generation [#183, Incremental]

Linguistics, Syntax, and Language Theory — avg: 44 (Moderately Innovative)

These papers ground language AI in formal linguistic and philosophical foundations. Arabic Diacritics examines orthographic marks' impact on NLP systems across domains. Discrete Distinguishability proposes logical systems on discrete domains rather than permissive continuous settings. Grammaticality as Kind debates the ontological status of linguistic categories. Together they challenge NLP to engage with foundational questions about linguistic representation and formal semantics.
  • Grammaticality as Kind: Ontology, Epistemology, and Empirical Pay-off [#128, Moderately Innovative]
  • Do Diacritics Matter? Evaluating the Impact of Arabic Diacritics on Tokenization and LLM Benchmarks [#157, Moderately Innovative]
  • Discrete Distinguishability as the Ontological Basis of Logic [#202, Incremental]

Recommendation Systems and Personalization — avg: 43 (Moderately Innovative)

These five papers address fundamental challenges in recommendation systems. E-commerce Personalization combines content-based and collaborative filtering to mitigate serendipity and cold-start problems. Venue Recommendation applies similar hybrid approaches to academic publishing. Adversarial MF uses GANs to improve collaborative filtering robustness. Customer Retention provides causal evidence on retention impact. PenRec addresses pension savings recommendations under information overload. Collectively they show recommendation systems maturing from single-modality (pure CF or content) to hybrid approaches addressing cold-start, sparsity, and user heterogeneity.
  • Sparse Contrastive Learning for Content-Based Cold Item Recommendation [#46, Innovative]
  • Effect of Personalized AI-Driven Recommendations on Customer Retention in E-Commerce Platforms [#127, Moderately Innovative]
  • Combining content information with collaborative filtering for publication venue recommendation [#173, Moderately Innovative]
  • PenRec: A Recommender System for Pension Savings [#179, Moderately Innovative]
  • An adversarial framework with dual genetic optimization for similarity-aware matrix factorization in recommendation systems [#195, Incremental]
  • Optimizing E-commerce Personalization through Hybrid Decision Tree–Nearest Neighbor Recommendation Integration [#218, Routine]

Financial Intelligence and Time Series Analysis — avg: 42 (Moderately Innovative)

These papers apply AI to financial forecasting and quantum computing benchmarking. Accelerating Financial Intelligence Distributed Systems augments time series forecasting with qualitative narrative analysis for regime shifts. QuanBench+ provides unified multi-framework quantum code generation benchmarking. Both address domains where diverse modalities (numerical + qualitative, or multiple frameworks) must be integrated.
  • PRAGMA: Revolut Foundation Model [#83, Innovative]
  • QuanBench+: A Unified Multi-Framework Benchmark for LLM-Based Quantum Code Generation [#156, Moderately Innovative]
  • Accelerating Financial Intelligence via High Throughput Distributed Systems for Large Language Model Augmented Time Series Forecasting [#219, Routine]

Knowledge Graphs and Semantic Integration — avg: 40 (Moderately Innovative)

These seven papers form a coherent narrative on integrating structured knowledge with neural models. KG Embedding for EHRs addresses heterogeneous clinical data integration. Hybrid Multi-Source RAG combines dense vector retrieval with relational reasoning over multiple sources. Text-to-SPARQL enables natural language queries over knowledge graphs. OQuaRE-KG establishes quality assurance frameworks for KGs themselves. KGBR demonstrates domain-specific KG construction for biomedical research. Ontology-Based RAGs leverage structured semantics in retrieval. Graphs Useful for LLMs? surveys graph-enhanced architectures. The convergence suggests knowledge graphs are becoming essential infrastructure for factual, explainable, and multimodal AI.
  • Are Graphs Useful for LLMs? A Comprehensive Survey of Graph-Enhanced Large Language Models [#101, Innovative]
  • Hybrid Multi-Source RAG for Context-Aware Conversational Data Access [#153, Moderately Innovative]
  • Combination of Ontology-Based Retrieval Augmented Graphs and Inductive Reasoning for Knowledge Fusion of Intelligent Systems [#172, Moderately Innovative]
  • Knowledge Graph Embedding and Alignment of Incomplete Electronic Health Records for Critical Care Applications [#180, Incremental]
  • OQuaRE-KG: an OQuaRE inspired framework for knowledge graph quality assessment [#188, Incremental]
  • KGBR: A Domain-Specific Knowledge Graph for Bone Regeneration Research [#204, Incremental]
  • Enhancing Text-to-SPARQL Generation via In-Context Learning with Example Selection Strategies [#211, Incremental]

Educational AI and Training Optimization — avg: 39 (Incremental)

Both papers address personalized learning at scale. Personalizing Education provides a framework for AI-driven individualization of instruction. AI Enhanced Optimization of PE Programs uses hybrid genetic algorithms and learning-based fitness evaluation for non-linear performance assessment. They reflect growing emphasis on adapting instruction to individual learner heterogeneity rather than uniform curricula.
  • Neural-Symbolic Knowledge Tracing: Injecting Educational Knowledge into Deep Learning for Responsible Learner Modelling [#87, Innovative]
  • Personalizing Education Through Artificial Intelligence: A Systematic Review and Experimental Framework [#201, Incremental]
  • AI Enhanced Optimization of College Physical Education Programs Using Hybrid Genetic Algorithms and Learning Based Fitness Evaluation [#214, Incremental]

Tabular Data and Structured Prediction — avg: 38 (Incremental)

Both papers address challenges in structured, tabular data. ODUTQA-MDC handles underspecified tabular queries requiring dialogue-based clarification. Survey on Tabular Data reviews tree-based and deep learning approaches for the dominant modality in healthcare and finance. They suggest tabular data demands specialized handling beyond generic neural architectures.
  • ODUTQA-MDC: A Task for Open-Domain Underspecified Tabular QA with Multi-turn Dialogue-based Clarification [#158, Moderately Innovative]
  • A Survey on Tabular Data: From Tree-based Methods to Tabular Deep Learning [#209, Incremental]

Data Integration and Semantic Interoperability — avg: 37 (Incremental)

These papers tackle semantic fragmentation across domains. BIM-IDS Framework enables syntactic and semantic data exchange in construction 5.0. FAIR Data Workflow implements FAIR principles for ion-exchange chromatography across interdisciplinary boundaries. Agrisemantics addresses agricultural data silos via semantic standards. Sustainability KGs integrate CO2, water, and social indicators into unified knowledge graphs. Semantic Enrichment extracts contextual meaning from heterogeneous big data. Spatial-Temporal KR unifies geographic, temporal, and foundational ontologies. They collectively reflect a shift toward domain-specific semantic standards and integrative knowledge representation.
  • Beyond CO2: Integrating Water, Social Indicators, and Uncertainty into Sustainability Knowledge Graphs [#97, Innovative]
  • FAIR Data Workflow Implementation and Assessment for Ion-Exchange Chromatography in Plasma Science [#154, Moderately Innovative]
  • Agrisemantics: The Current State of Adoption [#174, Moderately Innovative]
  • Adaptive BIM–IDS Framework for Semantic-Level Data Interoperability in Construction 5.0 Environments [#212, Incremental]
  • Semantic Enrichment Techniques in Big Data Analytics [#223, Routine]

Knowledge Representation and Ontology Design — avg: 36 (Incremental)

Both papers tackle systematic knowledge representation. KG4CUT formalizes cutting tool selection knowledge as a structured ontology enabling interoperability. Data Ontologies for Decision-Making help state DOTs convert accumulated data into actionable business assets. They suggest ontology engineering is becoming critical infrastructure for knowledge-intensive domains.
  • KG4CUT: an ontology to facilitate cutting tool selection and interoperability [#155, Moderately Innovative]
  • Data Ontologies for Data-Driven Decision-Making: Research Approach and Findings [#197, Incremental]
  • Spatial and Temporal Knowledge Representation: Ontological Foundations, Semantic Web Standards [#207, Incremental]

Mathematical Reasoning and Symbolic AI — avg: 31 (Incremental)

These papers bridge neural and symbolic approaches for mathematical problem-solving. Template-Theorems Graph Construction grounds mathematical reasoning in structured proof graphs. FGeo-ISRL combines MCTS with deep RL for geometry problem-solving under formal constraints. Neural Symbolic LSTM Regression recovers symbolic expressions from truncated Taylor series. Neuro-Symbolic AI with Edge Computing ensures constraint satisfaction in safety-critical design workflows. Collectively, they move beyond pure neural reasoning toward hybrid systems with formal guarantees.
  • FGeo-ISRL: A MCTS-Enhanced Deep Reinforcement Learning System for Plane Geometry Problem-Solving via Inverse Search [#116, Moderately Innovative]
  • Template-Theorems Graph Construction to Enhance Mathematical Reasoning Capabilities of LLM [#161, Moderately Innovative]
  • Neural Symbolic LSTM Regression from Truncated Taylor Series [#215, Routine]
  • Neuro-Symbolic AI with Edge Computing and Reinforcement Learning Optimizing Autonomous Engineering Design Workflows [#225, Routine]

Edge Computing and Mobile AI Systems — avg: 26 (Incremental)

These papers address deployment constraints and governance of AI at the edge. Mobile Assistants avoids cloud latency and privacy risks by running LLMs locally. Hybrid Edge-AI Framework combines neural and symbolic approaches to handle concept drift and regulatory compliance. Democratization of GenAI explores equitable, participatory futures for generative AI governance. They reflect tension between capability centralization (cloud) and decentralization (edge) balanced with governance and accessibility.
  • A hybrid self-evolving edge-AI framework for scalable digital process twins in enterprise systems [#184, Incremental]
  • Towards intelligent mobile on-device assistants: low-level text-to-actions with GPT LLMs [#208, Incremental]
  • Mapping the Responsible Democratization of Generative AI through Participatory Futuring [#221, Routine]

Applied AI in Specialized Domains — avg: 22 (Incremental)

These papers apply AI across manufacturing, environmental, behavioral, and pharmaceutical domains. AI in Science and Technology surveys broad integration across disciplines. Digital Technologies for Steelmaking addresses decarbonization via automated process control. Thessaly Flooding remediation applies AI to disaster recovery. Behavioral Data Representation Learning formalizes representation of dynamic human interactions. Indomethacin highlights overlooked drug candidates with antiviral potential. The group demonstrates AI's expanding footprint beyond software into physical systems, emergency response, and basic research.
  • International State of the Art Report: Digital Technologies for Steelmaking [#198, Incremental]
  • A Survey on Behavioral Data Representation Learning [#205, Incremental]
  • First Report Regarding Post-Disaster Remediation of 2023 Thessaly Flooding [#213, Incremental]
  • Indomethacin, a drug with known antiviral activity against SARS-CoVs neglected during the COVID-19 pandemic [#216, Routine]
  • AI in Science and Technology: Current Advances and Future Directions [#224, Routine]
